title: COMMUNICATION-INBOX
scope: Adds bi-directional landlord↔tenant message threads on top of Phase 28 [TENANT-PORTAL] notifications. Scout (2026-05-19) confirms greenfield on threads: TenantMessage (migration 2026_01_17_183438) is INBOUND-ONLY — it captures tenant replies from WhatsApp/SMS in response to outbound NotificationService notifications. InboxController in app/Http/Controllers/InboxController.php can only reply to received TenantMessage rows via the original channel; there is no model for landlord-initiated threads, no /tenant/inbox route on the tenant portal (only /tenant/notifications read-only), no "Message this tenant" CTA on landlord-side Pages/Tenants/Show.vue, and TenantAbilities::ABILITY_KEYS (app/Support/TenantAbilities.php:22-32) has no inbox:* keys. Strong primitives to extend: NotificationService::send accepts 12 type values + 5 channels; NotificationPreference matrix is type × channel boolean toggles with canReceive() gate; Reverb broadcasts on private notifications.{recipient_id} channel (routes/channels.php:20-22); Document polymorphic attachment (app/Models/Document.php:52-67) used by Phase 45 TICKET-PHOTOS for ticket annotations; Phase 62 offline-write layer has 5 named queues + resources/js/lib/offlineWriteQueue.ts pattern; lang/{en,sw,ar}/tenant.php enforced by tests/Feature/I18n/Phase24CiTest.php. Six categories × 3 = 18 findings closing the schema/compose/realtime/notify/moderation/CI gaps.

categories.INBOX-THREAD: Greenfield schema for bi-directional threads. (1) message_threads table with TenantScope (landlord_id) + polymorphic subject (subject_type / subject_id, nullable so standalone threads work) + status enum [open|archived|locked] + last_message_at index for sort-by-recency + MessageThread model with Auditable + SoftDeletes. (2) messages table (thread_id FK CASCADE, sender_id FK to users, body text, message_type enum [text|system|attachment], created_at index) + Message model with Auditable + SoftDeletes + parent-thread observer that updates MessageThread.last_message_at on insert. (3) message_thread_participants pivot (thread_id, user_id, last_read_at nullable, role enum [landlord|caretaker|tenant]) supporting multi-party threads (landlord + multiple caretakers + tenant) + MessageThread::scopeForUser($user) returns threads where the user appears in the participants pivot, NEVER by landlord_id alone (a tenant must not see other tenants' threads even within the same landlord).
categories.INBOX-COMPOSE: Landlord-initiated + tenant-initiated send surface with policies + attachments. (1) app/Http/Controllers/MessageThreadController (landlord-side: index / show / store / archive / lock) + app/Http/Controllers/Tenant/InboxController (tenant-side: index / show / store) + MessagePolicy / MessageThreadPolicy registered in AuthServiceProvider + Form Requests (StoreMessageThreadRequest, StoreMessageRequest) enforcing isLandlord() / isTenant() in authorize() + landlord_id + participant ownership invariant. (2) Polymorphic Document attachment via Message::documents() following Phase 45 TICKET-PHOTOS pattern (Document::create with documentable_type=Message::class) + StoreMessageRequest validates attachments array max:5 / mimes:jpeg,png,webp,pdf / max:5120 per file + MessageAttachmentService wrapping Storage::tenant() per Phase 58. (3) "Message this tenant" CTA on Pages/Tenants/Show.vue + new /tenant/inbox tenant route (role:tenant middleware) + Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Index.vue thread list + Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Show.vue thread detail with compose box + Pages/MessageThreads/Index.vue landlord thread list + Pages/MessageThreads/Show.vue thread detail.
categories.INBOX-REALTIME: Reverb push + read receipts + typing. (1) App\Events\MessagePosted broadcasts on PrivateChannel('inbox.thread.'.$thread_id) + routes/channels.php authorization checks $user is in $thread->participants pivot (NOT landlord_id-only — see INBOX-THREAD-3) + payload carries message_id + sender + body + created_at + version. (2) Read receipts via PATCH /messages/{message}/read endpoint updating message_thread_participants.last_read_at to NOW for the current user + MessageThread::unreadCountFor($user) accessor reading WHERE messages.created_at > participant.last_read_at + UI badges on Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Index.vue + landlord thread list rows. (3) Typing indicators via Echo client whisper API (Echo.private('inbox.thread.'.id).whisper('typing', { user_id, name }).listenForWhisper('typing', cb)) — whispers are NOT persisted, NOT broadcast through Laravel, ephemeral by design. Composables/useTypingIndicator.ts wraps the pattern + debounce + 3s timeout.
categories.INBOX-NOTIFY: Bridge unread messages into the existing NotificationService preference matrix. (1) Wire NotificationService::send($recipient, type='new_message', urgency='important', channel=ALL) inside the MessagePosted listener — fires fallback email/SMS/WhatsApp ONLY when recipient.last_active_at is older than 5 min AND NotificationPreference::canReceive('new_message', $channel) returns true. Prevents in-app spam when the user is actively reading inbox. (2) Add 'new_message' to App\Models\Notification::TYPES const (currently 12 entries at app/Models/Notification.php:13-36) + NotificationPreference matrix expansion via additive migration setting new_message column defaults to true for email + push + in_app, false for sms/whatsapp (Kenya cost-sensitive); Phase24CiTest i18n parity + Phase28CiTest tenant_abilities parity assertions extended; existing preferences NOT mutated. (3) messages:notify-unread-fallback every 15 min via Schedule::command timezone('Africa/Nairobi') walking thread participants WHERE last_read_at < (last_message_at - INTERVAL 15 MINUTE) AND no fallback dispatched in last 60 min (Cache::add 60-min idempotency keyed on thread_id+user_id) + emits inbox_unread_fallback_count gauge.
categories.INBOX-MOD: Moderation surface + retention + rate limiting. (1) Sender-initiated soft-delete-within-5-min (Message::canBeDeletedBy($user) returns sender_id===user.id && created_at > now()-5min) + admin/landlord thread lock (MessageThread::status='locked' blocks new messages via MessagePolicy::create) + every soft-delete + lock writes an audit_logs row via the Auditable trait. (2) Per-landlord configurable message retention (default 7 years per Kenya DPA, opt-in shorter via landlords.message_retention_days nullable column) + scheduled messages:enforce-retention daily 03:15 Africa/Nairobi following Phase 59 FILE-RETENTION pattern, soft-deleting messages older than retention; runbook documents legal-hold override via App\Support\LegalHoldRegistry. (3) Rate limiting via throttle:messages middleware (60/hour per user, lower than throttle:sensitive 30/min because legitimate inbox use peaks at ≈ 1 message/minute during back-and-forth) + body length cap 4000 chars enforced in StoreMessageRequest + minimal App\Support\MessageContentPolicy::isSpam($body) checks 3 obvious patterns (>5 identical URL repetitions / >50% non-printable chars / known spam-token list under config/inbox.php).
categories.INBOX-CI: (1) Phase63InboxSurfaceTest cross-category presence map — MessageThread + Message + MessageThreadParticipant models exist with TenantScope (where applicable) + SoftDeletes + Auditable; MessagePolicy + MessageThreadPolicy registered in AuthServiceProvider; TenantAbilities::ABILITY_KEYS contains inbox:thread_create + inbox:message_send; routes/web.php declares the 5 landlord MessageThreadController routes + 3 tenant InboxController routes all carrying role middleware; MessagePosted event class exists; Echo channel auth (channels.php) for inbox.thread.* references the participants pivot; messages:notify-unread-fallback + messages:enforce-retention scheduled; throttle:messages rate limiter registered. (2) Phase 62 offline-write queue extended with 6th queue pm-offline-messages — resources/js/sw.ts registerOfflinePost('/inbox/messages', 'pm-offline-messages') + useBackgroundSync.ts QUEUE_NAMES + RouteFamily type updated + Phase62OfflineWritesTest watchdog extended to include 'messages' family; offline conflict path tested via If-Match using Phase 62 RowVersion trait pattern; offline.md updated. (3) docs/runbooks/inbox.md NEW — thread lifecycle table (open→archived→locked) + participant role matrix + read-receipt + notification fallback flow + retention policy + legal-hold override; lang/{en,sw,ar}/inbox.php created with key parity enforced by Phase24CiTest (Arabic/Swahili get [TODO-ar] / [TODO-sw] markers per Phase 52 contract); alert-thresholds.md rows for inbox_unread_fallback_count + inbox_rate_limit_hits_count.

findings.title: message_threads table + MessageThread model with polymorphic subject + status state machine
findings.evidence: Scout: no bi-directional thread primitive exists in repo. app/Models/TenantMessage.php is one-way inbound (tenant replies from WhatsApp/SMS to outbound notifications). Without a dedicated MessageThread root, message rows cannot be archived/locked as a unit and there is no efficient sort-by-recency for an inbox list view. Polymorphic subject is required so a thread can attach to a Lease (rent dispute), Ticket (maintenance follow-up), or be standalone (general inquiry).
findings.remediation: Migration create_message_threads_table: id, landlord_id (FK + index), subject_type (string nullable, mirrors Document.documentable_type contract), subject_id (unsignedBigInt nullable), title (string 200 nullable, used when no subject), status (enum [open|archived|locked] default 'open'), last_message_at (timestamp index), created_at + updated_at + deleted_at. Composite index (landlord_id, status, last_message_at desc). app/Models/MessageThread.php uses TenantScope + Auditable + SoftDeletes traits + STATUSES const [Open|Archived|Locked] + relationships: subject() morphTo, messages() hasMany, participants() belongsToMany(User, message_thread_participants) + withPivot(last_read_at, role) + scopeOpenOnly() + scopeForLandlord($landlordId). Boot adds saving listener that updates last_message_at automatically on related Message creation (also wired via Message::booted creating hook below).

findings.title: messages table + Message model + Message::booted creating hook bumps parent thread last_message_at
findings.evidence: Without an explicit Message model + thread linkage, the system has no way to (a) enforce sender ownership / participant membership at insert time, (b) maintain MessageThread.last_message_at for the inbox sort, or (c) audit individual message events via the Auditable trait. The scope-by-participant invariant is also load-bearing for cross-tenant isolation — a TenantScope on landlord_id alone is NOT sufficient since multiple tenants within the same landlord must not see each other's threads.
findings.remediation: Migration create_messages_table: id, thread_id (FK message_threads ON DELETE CASCADE + index), sender_id (FK users + index), body (text), message_type (enum [text|system|attachment] default 'text'), created_at index, updated_at, deleted_at. app/Models/Message.php uses Auditable + SoftDeletes (NOT TenantScope directly — messages inherit isolation from MessageThread via the participants pivot). Relationships: thread() belongsTo, sender() belongsTo(User), documents() morphMany via Document::documentable. Message::booted() creating hook updates parent MessageThread::last_message_at via $thread->update(['last_message_at' => $this->created_at ?? now()]) inside a DB::transaction to keep the inbox sort coherent under concurrent inserts. canBeDeletedBy($user) helper returns sender_id === $user->id && $this->created_at > now()->subMinutes(5) (used by MessagePolicy::delete in INBOX-MOD-1).

findings.title: message_thread_participants pivot + scopeForUser() participant-based isolation
findings.evidence: A thread between landlord + tenant A must not be visible to tenant B even when they share landlord_id. TenantScope on MessageThread.landlord_id alone leaks cross-tenant visibility. Multi-party threads (landlord + multiple caretakers responding to a ticket) need an explicit join table to capture per-participant last_read_at for read receipts.
findings.remediation: Migration create_message_thread_participants_table: id, thread_id (FK CASCADE), user_id (FK users), role (enum [landlord|caretaker|tenant]), last_read_at (timestamp nullable), created_at + updated_at, unique(thread_id, user_id). app/Models/MessageThreadParticipant.php pivot model (extends Pivot). MessageThread::scopeForUser($query, User $user) joins message_thread_participants on thread_id where user_id = $user->id — used by every landlord + tenant inbox query so cross-tenant isolation falls out of the join. System messages (message_type='system') for events like 'Caretaker added to thread' / 'Thread locked by landlord' emitted via MessageThread::recordSystemEvent($event, $metadata) helper that inserts a Message with sender_id NULL + message_type='system'. Watchdog Phase63ThreadTest::test_cross_tenant_isolation_via_participants asserts that two tenants under the same landlord cannot enumerate each other's threads.

findings.title: MessageThreadController + Tenant\InboxController + policies + Form Requests
findings.evidence: No landlord compose surface exists today. InboxController only replies to received TenantMessage (WhatsApp/SMS ingest). Without policies + Form Requests the route layer is exposed to landlord_id spoofing, cross-thread leakage, and role-confused writes (landlord posting as tenant).
findings.remediation: app/Http/Controllers/MessageThreadController (landlord): index / show / store / archive / lock + role:landlord,caretaker middleware. app/Http/Controllers/Tenant/InboxController: index / show / store + role:tenant middleware. app/Policies/MessageThreadPolicy: viewAny (Inertia inbox list) / view (must appear in participants pivot OR be landlord owner) / create (landlord+caretaker create; tenants only via existing-thread reply OR via initiate-to-landlord flow) / archive (landlord only) / lock (landlord only). app/Policies/MessagePolicy: view (parent thread MessageThreadPolicy::view) / create (parent thread is open AND user is participant) / delete (Message::canBeDeletedBy()). Form Requests: StoreMessageThreadRequest validates subject_type Rule::in(['lease','ticket', null]) + subject_id Rule::exists scoped to landlord_id + participants array of user_ids Rule::exists scoped to landlord_id + initial message body required min:1 max:4000; StoreMessageRequest validates body required min:1 max:4000 + attachments array max:5 + parent thread is open. Both Form Request authorize() methods call $this->user()->can('create', [Message::class, $thread]).

findings.title: Polymorphic Document attachment on Message via Storage::tenant() + 5-file 5MB cap
findings.evidence: Phase 45 TICKET-PHOTOS shipped polymorphic Document attachment for ticket annotations using Storage::disk('local')->store + Document::create with documentable_type=Ticket::class. Inbox attachments need the same shape but routed through Storage::tenant() per Phase 58 SHARED-DISK-MIGRATION so an s3 cutover survives without code edits. Without size + count caps the inbox is a DoS + storage-cost vector.
findings.remediation: app/Services/Inbox/MessageAttachmentService::attachToMessage(Message $message, array $files): void wraps Storage::tenant()->putFileAs() + Document::create with documentable_type=Message::class + documentable_id=$message->id + landlord_id derived from $message->thread->landlord_id. Path template: messages/{thread_id}/{message_id}/{ulid}.{ext} routed through PrefixedDisk per Phase 59. StoreMessageRequest gates attachments at max:5 / mimes:jpeg,png,webp,pdf / max:5120 (5MB). Files are scanned for MIME mismatch via finfo_file before persistence (Phase 5 FILE-UPLOAD pattern). Failed scans throw with i18n 'inbox.attachment.invalid_mime'. Documents inherit SoftDeletes so retention sweeps + sender-soft-delete-within-5min naturally cascade.

findings.title: Vue surface: landlord thread list/detail + tenant inbox list/detail + "Message this tenant" CTA
findings.evidence: Tenant portal has /tenant/notifications (read-only) but no /tenant/inbox. Pages/Tenants/Show.vue (landlord-side tenant detail) has no "Message this tenant" entry point — landlords today can only contact tenants via the outbound notification pipeline which doesn't support free-form messages.
findings.remediation: New Pages/MessageThreads/Index.vue (landlord thread list, paginated via LimitsPerPage trait, filter by status + by participant tenant) + Pages/MessageThreads/Show.vue (thread detail with message list + compose textarea + attachment dropzone + archive/lock dropdown). New Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Index.vue + Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Show.vue mirror structure (no archive/lock for tenants). Pages/Tenants/Show.vue gains a "Message" button next to existing CTAs (visible when can('inbox:thread_create')) that POSTs to MessageThreadController::store with the tenant pre-populated as participant + opens the new thread. routes/web.php declares /message-threads (landlord) + /tenant/inbox (tenant) route groups both inside the existing role middleware blocks per Phase 28 contract. AuthenticatedLayout topbar gains an inbox bell icon (mirrors NotificationBell.vue) showing unread thread count via TenantAbilities + AuthAbilities lookup.

findings.title: MessagePosted broadcast event + participant-aware channel auth
findings.evidence: Existing channels.php (routes/channels.php:20-22) authorizes notifications.{userId} via simple identity match. Inbox channels need participant-pivot membership check — a wrongly-authorized channel leaks message bodies to non-participants in real time even when REST endpoints are locked down.
findings.remediation: app/Events/MessagePosted implements ShouldBroadcast + broadcastOn returns PrivateChannel('inbox.thread.'.$this->message->thread_id) + broadcastAs 'message.posted' + broadcastWith returns slim DTO { message_id, thread_id, sender: {id, name, role}, body, message_type, created_at, version }. Sender is excluded via toOthers() so the originator's optimistic UI isn't duplicated. routes/channels.php registers Broadcast::channel('inbox.thread.{threadId}', function (User $user, int $threadId) { return DB::table('message_thread_participants')->where('thread_id', $threadId)->where('user_id', $user->id)->exists(); }) — NEVER landlord_id-based. Phase63RealtimeTest::test_channel_auth_blocks_non_participant asserts a user who is NOT in the participants pivot is denied even when same landlord_id.

findings.title: Read receipts via PATCH /messages/{message}/read + last_read_at + unreadCountFor()
findings.evidence: Without read receipts, users have no signal whether their message was seen — a core trust feature for landlord/tenant relationships. Unread count is also load-bearing for the inbox bell + per-thread badge in Pages/MessageThreads/Index.vue + Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Index.vue.
findings.remediation: PATCH /messages/{message}/read endpoint on both MessageThreadController + Tenant\InboxController updates message_thread_participants.last_read_at = $message->created_at for the current user WHERE user is in pivot for $message->thread_id. Idempotent (no-op if last_read_at >= $message->created_at). MessageThread::unreadCountFor(User $user): int returns Message::where('thread_id', $this->id)->where('created_at', '>', participant.last_read_at)->where('sender_id', '!=', $user->id)->count() (excludes user's own messages). HandleInertiaRequests::share() adds auth.inbox_unread_total computed from sum of unreadCountFor across all threads where user is participant (Cache::remember 30s keyed on user_id). Pages/MessageThreads/Index.vue + Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Index.vue badge each row with per-thread unread count.

findings.title: Typing indicators via Echo client whisper + useTypingIndicator composable
findings.evidence: Typing indicators are a UX nicety, not load-bearing. Implementing them via server-broadcast (push every keystroke through Reverb) is wasteful; the Echo whisper API is purpose-built for ephemeral client-to-client events.
findings.remediation: resources/js/composables/useTypingIndicator.ts wraps Echo.private('inbox.thread.'+threadId).whisper('typing', { user_id, name }) on keystroke (debounced 500ms) + .listenForWhisper('typing', cb) on mount + clears the indicator after 3s of silence. Pages/MessageThreads/Show.vue + Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Show.vue render '{Name} is typing…' below the message list when receivedTyping.value !== null. Whispers are NOT persisted, NOT routed through Laravel — purely client-to-client through the Reverb channel. No backend changes required beyond Phase63RealtimeTest::test_whisper_event_does_not_persist (confirms no messages table row is created from a whisper).

findings.title: MessagePosted listener wires NotificationService::send type='new_message' fallback for offline recipients
findings.evidence: If recipient is not actively viewing inbox, the Reverb push is silent — no email/SMS/WhatsApp goes out. Users miss messages. Conversely, if recipient IS active, a fallback dispatch creates inbox-spam (read the message twice: in real time + in email).
findings.remediation: app/Listeners/SendUnreadMessageFallback implements ShouldQueue + Phase-16 backoff. On MessagePosted handle(), iterates participants where user_id != sender_id; for each, checks $user->last_active_at < now()->subMinutes(5) (HandleInertiaRequests already touches last_active_at on every Inertia request); if so, calls NotificationService::send($user, type='new_message', urgency='important', payload=[thread_id, message_id, sender_name, body_preview=Str::limit($body, 120)]). NotificationService internally consults NotificationPreference::canReceive('new_message', $channel) per channel (email/sms/whatsapp/push/in_app) so opt-outs are honored automatically. Idempotent via Cache::add 'new_message:fallback:'.$message->id 5min lock to prevent listener double-fire under retry.

findings.title: Add 'new_message' to Notification::TYPES + additive NotificationPreference column + parity tests
findings.evidence: NotificationService::send rejects unknown type values via the type allowlist at app/Models/Notification.php:13-36 (12 entries: rent_reminder, arrears_notice, invoice, receipt, rent_hike, lease_expiry, lease_renewal, maintenance_notice, general, eviction_notice, caretaker_invitation, tenant_invitation). NotificationPreference matrix has one boolean column per type × channel — adding new_message requires an additive migration. Phase24CiTest enforces key parity across en/sw/ar locales and Phase28CiTest enforces TenantAbilities + i18n consistency — both must be updated atomically with the type addition or CI breaks.
findings.remediation: (a) app/Models/Notification.php: add 'new_message' to TYPES const at position 13 (preserves enum order). (b) Additive migration phase63_add_new_message_to_notification_preferences: ADD COLUMN new_message_email BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE, new_message_push BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE, new_message_in_app BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE, new_message_sms BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, new_message_whatsapp BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE (Kenya cost-sensitive defaults: free channels on, paid channels off). NotificationPreference::canReceive() already reads dynamic column names; no code change needed there. (c) lang/{en,sw,ar}/notifications.php: add new_message.title + new_message.body templates (ar/sw get [TODO-ar]/[TODO-sw] markers per Phase 52 contract). (d) Phase24CiTest assertions extended; Phase28CiTest tenant_abilities parity untouched (this finding is notify-side only — abilities updated in INBOX-CI-1 with the inbox:* keys). (e) NO data migration on existing notification_preferences rows beyond the column default — backfill happens implicitly via the DEFAULT clause.

findings.title: messages:notify-unread-fallback cron + quiet-hours-respecting digest
findings.evidence: Even with the listener-side fallback (NOTIFY-1), a user who reads the in-app message THEN logs off without responding doesn't trigger any fallback (last_active_at stays recent). A digest cron catches the trailing case: messages sent while recipient was active but never replied to.
findings.remediation: app/Console/Commands/MessagesNotifyUnreadFallback every 15 min via Schedule::command->timezone('Africa/Nairobi')->onOneServer(). Walks message_thread_participants WHERE last_read_at IS NULL OR last_read_at < (corresponding MessageThread.last_message_at - INTERVAL 15 MINUTE) AND (now() - MessageThread.last_message_at) BETWEEN 15min AND 24h (skip very-fresh + very-old). For each, NotificationPreference::canReceive() check + Cache::add 'new_message:digest:'.$thread_id.':'.$user_id 60min idempotency lock so a slow-replying user isn't paged every 15 min. Honors NotificationPreference quiet_hours_enabled/start/end (existing field set, NotificationService already consults). Emits inbox_unread_fallback_count gauge for ops dashboards.

findings.title: Sender soft-delete-within-5-min + landlord thread lock + audit trail
findings.evidence: Without a delete affordance, users have no way to retract typos or accidental sends — a basic UX expectation in any modern messaging surface. Without lock, landlords have no kill-switch on abusive/harassing threads. Both actions must write to audit_logs for DPA-3 lawful-basis tagging.
findings.remediation: Message::canBeDeletedBy(User $user): bool returns sender_id === $user->id && created_at > now()->subMinutes(5). MessagePolicy::delete delegates to canBeDeletedBy. DELETE /messages/{message} on both MessageThreadController + Tenant\InboxController calls $message->delete() (SoftDeletes) + emits a system Message ('Message deleted by sender') so the recipient sees the gap. MessageThread::lock() updates status to 'locked' + emits system Message ('Thread locked by landlord') + writes audit_logs row. MessagePolicy::create returns false when parent thread status === 'locked'. POST /message-threads/{thread}/lock + /unlock landlord-only routes (gate via MessageThreadPolicy::lock). Auditable trait already wires audit_logs writes on every model save — verify the trait is on Message + MessageThread; system messages get lawful_basis='legitimate_interest' per Phase 13 DPA-3.

findings.title: Per-landlord message retention + messages:enforce-retention cron
findings.evidence: Kenya DPA defaults to 7-year retention for landlord-tenant communications (lease-adjacent records). Without scheduled enforcement, the messages table grows unbounded. Per-landlord override is needed for landlords who want stricter retention than the platform default.
findings.remediation: Additive migration phase63_add_message_retention_to_landlords: ADD COLUMN message_retention_days INTEGER UNSIGNED NULL (NULL = platform default 7 years). app/Console/Commands/MessagesEnforceRetention daily 03:15 Africa/Nairobi follows Phase 59 FILE-RETENTION pattern: chunk by landlord_id; for each landlord compute effective retention (column value OR config('inbox.retention.default_days', 2557 for 7 years)); soft-delete messages WHERE thread.landlord_id = X AND messages.created_at < now()->subDays($retention) AND NOT in App\Support\LegalHoldRegistry::heldThreadIds(). Emits messages_enforce_retention_deleted_count gauge per landlord. docs/runbooks/inbox.md documents the LegalHoldRegistry override for dispute / litigation scenarios.

findings.title: throttle:messages rate limiter + 4000-char body cap + minimal spam guard
findings.evidence: Without rate limiting, a compromised account can flood the inbox surface (DoS the notification pipeline + cost in SMS/WhatsApp fallback). Without body cap, the messages table is exposed to arbitrary-size text writes. Without minimal spam guard, a basic 'URL repetition' attack lands in every tenant inbox.
findings.remediation: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider::boot adds RateLimiter::for('messages', fn (Request $request) => Limit::perMinute(20)->by($request->user()->id)) — 60/hr effective via the per-minute cap; lower than throttle:sensitive (5/min) because legitimate inbox use peaks at ≈ 1 msg/min during back-and-forth. Applied to POST /message-threads/* + POST /tenant/inbox/* via throttle:messages middleware. StoreMessageRequest body rule: required|string|min:1|max:4000. app/Support/MessageContentPolicy::isSpam(string $body): bool checks (a) >5 identical http(s) URL repetitions, (b) >50% non-printable characters (mb_check_encoding 'UTF-8' + count of \p{C} via preg_match_all), (c) presence of any term in config/inbox.php['spam_tokens'] (default empty list, operator-curated). StoreMessageRequest fails closed with ValidationException + i18n 'inbox.message.spam_rejected'. Emits inbox_rate_limit_hits_count + inbox_spam_rejected_count gauges.

findings.title: Phase63InboxSurfaceTest cross-category presence map
findings.evidence: Past cycles (Phase 28 Phase28TenantSurfaceTest, Phase 50 Phase50ReportsDepthSurfaceTest, Phase 55 Phase55DashboardDepthSurfaceTest) all ship a single watchdog test asserting every category's surface artifacts exist — catches accidental deletion / refactor drift in one place.
findings.remediation: tests/Feature/Phase63InboxSurfaceTest with these assertions: (a) MessageThread + Message + MessageThreadParticipant model files exist + extend the expected base + use the expected traits (TenantScope on MessageThread, Auditable + SoftDeletes on both Message + MessageThread, Pivot on MessageThreadParticipant); (b) MessagePolicy + MessageThreadPolicy registered in AuthServiceProvider::$policies; (c) TenantAbilities::ABILITY_KEYS contains 'inbox:thread_create' + 'inbox:message_send' + 'inbox:thread_archive'(landlord-only); AuthAbilities mirrors with same keys; (d) routes/web.php exposes the 5 landlord MessageThreadController routes + 3 tenant InboxController routes, each carrying role:landlord,caretaker or role:tenant; (e) MessagePosted event class exists + implements ShouldBroadcast; (f) Broadcast::channel call for 'inbox.thread.{threadId}' registered; (g) Schedule registry contains messages:notify-unread-fallback every 15min + messages:enforce-retention daily 03:15 Africa/Nairobi; (h) RateLimiter::for('messages') registered.

findings.title: Phase 62 offline-write queue extended with pm-offline-messages (6th queue)
findings.evidence: Phase 62 OFFLINE-WRITES shipped 5 named queues (invoices, tickets, comments, readings, payments). Messages are a natural 6th — a tenant or landlord composing on a slow / disconnected mobile expects their message to land when connectivity returns. Without this, the inbox feels broken on Kenyan rural networks.
findings.remediation: resources/js/sw.ts: registerOfflinePost(/\/message-threads\/\d+\/messages$/, 'pm-offline-messages') + registerOfflinePost(/\/tenant\/inbox\/\d+\/messages$/, 'pm-offline-messages'). resources/js/composables/useBackgroundSync.ts: QUEUE_NAMES gains 'messages' → 'pm-offline-messages' + RouteFamily type gains 'messages'. Pages/MessageThreads/Show.vue + Pages/Tenant/Inbox/Show.vue compose form passes routeFamily:'messages' to useBackgroundSync. Phase62OfflineWritesTest::test_route_families watchdog extended to assert 'messages' is in the families list. Conflict path (offline replay hits a locked thread) uses Phase 62 RowVersion trait pattern — If-Match header sourced from cached MessageThread.version (added via the same migration as the new_message column for surface coherence; thread row gets version unsignedInteger default 1). offline.md updated with the messages queue + conflict resolution flow.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/inbox.md + lang/{en,sw,ar}/inbox.php + alert-thresholds rows
findings.evidence: Past cycles all ship a domain runbook + locale parity (Phase 49 maintenance.md, Phase 50 reports.md, Phase 55 dashboard.md). Without this the operator has no documented procedure for thread lock / legal-hold / retention overrides + i18n CI breaks on the new keys.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/inbox.md sections: (a) Thread lifecycle (open→archived→locked, who can transition each); (b) Participant roles (landlord/caretaker/tenant + system); (c) Read-receipt flow + unread-fallback timing; (d) Notification fallback decision tree (5min last_active_at gate + per-channel preference + quiet-hours + idempotency); (e) Retention + LegalHoldRegistry override + dispute scenarios; (f) Rate limits + body caps + spam tokens; (g) Offline queue + conflict resolution. lang/en/inbox.php + lang/sw/inbox.php + lang/ar/inbox.php with parity — ar/sw populated with [TODO-ar]/[TODO-sw] markers per Phase 52 contract; Phase24CiTest extended to include the new namespace. docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md gets two new rows: inbox_unread_fallback_count (sev3 if > 1000/15min cluster-wide indicates Reverb outage) + inbox_rate_limit_hits_count (sev4, visibility-only).

execution_plan.phase_1a: INBOX-THREAD-1/2/3 — migration + models + scopeForUser + system-message helper. Tests: Phase63ThreadTest (schema, scope isolation, system events).
execution_plan.phase_1b: INBOX-COMPOSE-1/2/3 — controllers + policies + Form Requests + Vue surface + 'Message this tenant' CTA. Tests: Phase63ComposeTest (authz, attachment caps, Inertia props).
execution_plan.phase_1c: INBOX-REALTIME-1/2/3 — MessagePosted event + channel auth + read-receipt endpoint + typing whisper composable. Tests: Phase63RealtimeTest (channel auth, whisper non-persistence, unread counts).
execution_plan.phase_1d: INBOX-NOTIFY-1/2/3 — SendUnreadMessageFallback listener + TYPES expansion + preferences migration + digest cron + i18n keys. Tests: Phase63NotifyTest (listener gate logic, idempotency, parity).
execution_plan.phase_1e: INBOX-MOD-1/2/3 — sender delete + thread lock + retention cron + rate limiter + content policy. Tests: Phase63ModTest (delete window, lock state machine, retention cron, rate limit, spam guard).
execution_plan.phase_2: INBOX-CI-1/2/3 — Phase63InboxSurfaceTest + offline queue extension + inbox.md runbook + lang parity. Closeout JSON merge + memory + path_forward + MEMORY.md update.

commit_lineage: 735250c — INBOX-THREAD-1/2/3 (message_threads + messages + participants pivot, scopeForUser participant-based isolation, system-message helper, canBeDeletedBy 5-min window, Message::booted last_message_at bump)
22eff74 — INBOX-COMPOSE-1/2/3 (MessageThreadController + Tenant\InboxController + policies + Form Requests + MessageAttachmentService via Storage::tenant() + 4 Vue scaffold pages + routes + i18n)
fda1a1a — INBOX-REALTIME-1/2/3 (MessagePosted ShouldBroadcast + participant-pivot channel auth + PATCH /messages/{id}/read + useTypingIndicator composable + auth.inbox_unread_total Inertia share)

fdb0ee8 — INBOX-NOTIFY-1/2/3 (SendUnreadMessageFallback ShouldQueue listener gated on last_active_at + Notification::TYPE_NEW_MESSAGE + NotificationPreference new_message_enabled + messages:notify-unread-fallback digest cron + HandleInertiaRequests debounced presence touch)

71b134d — INBOX-MOD-1/2/3 (MessageDeleteController invokable + MessageThreadModerationController archive/lock/unlock + users.message_retention_days + messages:enforce-retention cron + RateLimiter::for('messages') 20/min + throttle:messages on all 4 POST routes + MessageContentPolicy::isSpam URL-repetition / non-printable heuristics)

f4df96c — INBOX-CI-1/2/3 (Phase63InboxSurfaceTest cross-category presence map + pm-offline-messages 6th queue in sw.ts + useBackgroundSync + docs/runbooks/inbox.md + alert-thresholds rows + notifications.type ENUM extended with new_message)
sub_scope_deferrals: 'Message this tenant' CTA on Pages/Tenants/Show.vue + AuthenticatedLayout inbox bell icon: the controller + route + policy contract ship, but the actual button-mount on existing landlord-side Vue pages is deferred to a Vue-tail cycle (same pattern as Phase 51 VUE-TAIL-1 for Phase 42/45/46/47/48/50 deferrals).

LegalHoldRegistry implementation: docs/runbooks/inbox.md describes the seam at messages:enforce-retention command but no App\Support\LegalHoldRegistry class actually ships. Deferred until first real legal-hold / litigation scenario surfaces a concrete shape.

Vue UI polish — message-list virtualization for very long threads, attachment thumbnail preview, drag-and-drop file upload UX, typing-indicator animation: scaffolds are functional but visual depth is deferred to a future polish cycle.
carry_overs_opened: INBOX-VUE-TAIL: 'Message this tenant' CTA mount on Pages/Tenants/Show.vue + inbox bell icon on AuthenticatedLayout + UI polish (virtualization / attachment thumbnails / drag-and-drop / typing animation). Estimated 1 sub-phase of a future Vue-tail cycle (the natural successor to Phase 51 VUE-TAIL-1 + the open A9 VUE-TAIL-OFFLINE).
